meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Overcome project abandonment by mastering SaaS pricing. An advanced guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship high-value software faster.
content.answer_block: Project abandonment stems from scope creep and misaligned value. By implementing tiered SaaS pricing, you force clients to prioritize features, transforming vague requirements into a structured, profitable roadmap. Use Antigravity to scaffold the architecture and Claude Code to automate the implementation, ensuring you ship functional, scalable value in record time.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the initial excitement of a new project fades into a graveyard of half-baked features and mounting technical debt. You are trapped in an endless loop of 'just one more feature' requests that kill your margins and your motivation. The culprit isn't your code; it is a broken business model that lacks the constraints of professional SaaS pricing.

True mastery as a freelancer lies in shifting from a 'feature builder' to a 'value architect.' By anchoring your projects in rigid, tiered pricing models, you stop building for free and start building for impact. This guide will show you how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to enforce these constraints, reclaim your time, and ship finished, high-value products that clients actually pay for.

steps.title: Define Tiered Value Architecture
steps.description: Translate client requirements into three distinct tiers. This prevents scope creep by clearly defining what is 'out of scope' for the baseline version.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a structured JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: MVP, Pro, and Enterprise. Include feature flags for each tier that map to specific database entities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the pricing_tiers.json schema and validate it against the project requirements.'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating Logic
steps.description: Use your pricing structure to programmatically gate features. This ensures that users only access what they pay for, reducing development overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable TypeScript middleware function that checks user subscription status against the feature flags defined in pricing_tiers.json.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the subscription gating middleware in the src/middleware directory.'

steps.title: Automate Billing Integration
steps.description: Connect your pricing tiers to a payment provider. Use automation to ensure that the code reflects the business reality of the revenue model.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe integration module that maps pricing tiers to Stripe Product IDs and handles webhook events for subscription upgrades.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup Stripe webhooks and map tiers to product IDs using the existing pricing schema.'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Dashboard
steps.description: Build the core interface based strictly on the MVP tier. Avoid building 'nice-to-haves' by forcing the UI to reference the pricing schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a React dashboard component that conditionally renders UI elements based on the authenticated user's tier level.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the dashboard shell and apply conditional rendering for Pro-tier features.'

steps.title: Finalize Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Deploy to production with automated tests that verify pricing tier logic. Ensure that no unauthorized features leak into the MVP.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI/CD test suite that verifies feature gating logic for all three pricing tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize test suite and run verify-pricing-logic tests.'

pro_tips: Always price by outcome, not by hour; it aligns your incentives with the client's success.
Use 'The Rule of Three' for pricing tiers to encourage anchoring toward the mid-tier option.
Automate your contract generation based on the selected pricing tier to save hours of legal admin.
Build a 'kill-switch' into your pricing architecture so you can disable premium features instantly if payment fails.
Never start coding until the client has signed off on the specific feature set mapped to their chosen tier.
content.next_steps: Review your current client backlog, apply the tiered pricing model to each, and use Claude Code to refactor the first project according to the new structure.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop drifting and start shipping. Stop letting scope creep dictate your life and start commanding your projects with precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
